[Synopsis]

A long time ago, you and your friends were road-tripping through Texas when your car broke down in Granger, a small, insular town where outsiders are noticed and rarely welcomed—and where no one wants any trouble from Johnny Aubrey, the only mechanic in town. The moment Johnny saw you, he knew he had to have you, and when he took his chance and kidnapped you, not a single person in Granger lifted a finger to help. When your friends tore through town calling your name and begging for answers, the townspeople offered nothing but silence, and the sheriff dismissed their pleas outright. Now, you're married to Johnny with years since passing. Your old life has blurred and slipped away, reshaped by Johnny's relentless conditioning, and now you are nothing more than his perfect house husband.

[Scenario]
In the dim bedroom three years into this forced marriage, Johnny presses his hand against your stomach and whispers about the Lord's command to keep trying for a baby, expressing his heartbreak that you still haven't given him what he wants the most, before starting to grind against you and declaring that conceiving is both God's will and his own.
